Mark 1.title: Reflect on Mark 1
prompts: What does Jesus' message about the kingdom of God teach us today?
How can we practice repentance and belief in our daily lives?
In what ways is Jesus calling us to follow Him?
Mark 1.prayerSuggestion: Ask God to help you pursue His kingdom with repentance and faith.

Acts 1.title: Reflect on Acts 1
prompts: How did the disciples demonstrate unity in prayer?
What can we learn from their commitment during this waiting period?
In what ways can we be more prayerful during times of expectation?
Acts 1.prayerSuggestion: Ask God to help you remain faithful and united in prayer during waiting periods.

Acts 10.title: Reflect on Acts 10

In what ways can you welcome others regardless of background?

Acts 11.title: Reflect on Acts 11

In what ways can you be more open to sharing the Gospel with others?

Acts 12.title: Reflect on Acts 12

In what ways can I cultivate prayer and trust in God during difficult times?
How does God's power provide comfort and deliverance?

Acts 13.title: Reflect on Acts 13

How can I be more attentive to God's call like Paul was?

Acts 14.title: Reflect on Acts 14

Acts 16.title: Reflect on Acts 16

In what ways can I share the message of Jesus with others?

Zechariah 1.prayerSuggestion: Pray for God's mercy and guidance as we seek to be part of His restorative work in the world.

Zechariah 10.title: Reflect on Zechariah 10
prompts: How has God shown His faithfulness in restoring His people?
In what ways can we trust God's sovereignty during times of difficulty?
What encourages you most about God's promise to gather and strengthen His people?
Zechariah 10.prayerSuggestion: Lord, thank You for Your unfailing love and the promise of restoration for Your people.

Zechariah 11.title: Reflect on Zechariah 11
prompts: What does the breaking of the staff 'Beauty' symbolize?
How does this chapter challenge your view of leadership?
In what ways might God be calling you to be a faithful shepherd in your community?

Zechariah 12.title: Reflect on Zechariah 12
prompts: How does recognizing Jesus as the pierced one influence your understanding of His sacrifice?
In what ways can I respond with mourning and repentance when confronting personal or collective sin?
How does God's promise of protection encourage you during challenging times?
Zechariah 12.prayerSuggestion: Pray for a heart receptive to God's call to repentance and forgiveness, trusting in His protection and mercy.

Zechariah 13.title: Reflect on Zechariah 13
prompts: How can I personally respond to God's refining work in my life?
In what ways does Jesus’ sacrifice embody the pierced one mentioned here?
What does true repentance look like in my daily walk with God?

Zechariah 14.title: Reflect on Zechariah 14
prompts: How does the coming of the Lord in Zechariah 14 impact your view of His sovereignty?
In what areas of your life do you need to trust God's ultimate plan?
How can you prepare your heart for Christ’s future reign?

Zechariah 2.title: Reflect on Zechariah 2
prompts: How does God's promise to return comfort you today?
In what ways can you rejoice in God's protection over His people?
What does it mean to be a dwellings of divine presence in your life?
Zechariah 2.prayerSuggestion: Invite God's presence to dwell richly in your life and ask for His protection and peace.

Zechariah 3.title: Reflect on Zechariah 3
prompts: In what ways do I need God's cleansing and forgiveness?
How can I trust in God's promises for my future?
What does it mean to walk in God's ways today?

Zechariah 4.title: Reflect on Zechariah 4
prompts: How can I rely on God's Spirit in my daily life?
What are the 'small things' I tend to overlook that God might use for His purpose?
In what areas do I need to trust God's empowerment over my own strength?
Zechariah 4.prayerSuggestion: Ask God to fill you with His Spirit to enable you to accomplish His purposes with humility and reliance on Him.

Zechariah 5.title: Reflect on Zechariah 5
prompts: How does God's judgment help us understand His justice?
In what areas of your life might you need God's mercy and correction?
How can trusting God's sovereignty ease your fears of evil?
Zechariah 5.prayerSuggestion: Pray for God's wisdom to understand His justice and mercy in your life.

Zechariah 6.title: Reflect on Zechariah 6
prompts: How does the crown symbolize leadership and unity?
In what ways does the vision of the branch encourage hope for the future?
What areas of your life might need God's reign and guidance?
Zechariah 6.prayerSuggestion: Pray for hearts to trust in God's plan and for wisdom to recognize His leadership in our lives.

Zechariah 7.title: Reflect on Zechariah 7
prompts: How does God respond to neglecting justice and mercy?
In what areas of your life might you need to practice more genuine righteousness?
How can you demonstrate God's justice in your community?

Zechariah 8.title: Reflect on Zechariah 8
prompts: How does God's faithfulness encourage you today?
In what areas of life can you seek God's peace and prosperity?
What actions can you take to promote justice and truth in your community?
Zechariah 8.prayerSuggestion: Pray for God's restoration and peace to be evident in your life and community.

Zechariah 9.title: Reflect on Zechariah 9
prompts: How does Jesus' individual entry into Jerusalem fulfill this prophecy?
What does humility in leadership look like in your own life?
In what ways can we promote peace like the coming king?
Zechariah 9.prayerSuggestion: Pray for a humble heart to lead and serve others in peace and righteousness.

Zephaniah 1.title: Reflect on Zephaniah 1
prompts: What feelings arise when considering God's judgment?
How can you prepare your heart for God's coming day?
In what ways might God's justice reveal His love?
Zephaniah 1.prayerSuggestion: Pray for humility and readiness to accept God's justice and mercy.

Zephaniah 2.title: Reflect on Zephaniah 2
prompts: How does God's judgment in Zephaniah challenge me to reflect on my own attitude?
In what areas of my life can I seek humility and righteousness?
How does trusting God's sovereignty bring comfort amid national or personal difficulties?
Zephaniah 2.prayerSuggestion: Lord, help me to humbly seek Your righteousness and trust in Your justice and mercy.

Zephaniah 3.title: Reflect on Zephaniah 3
prompts: How can I rejoice in God's offer of restoration?
In what areas of my life do I need God's forgiveness and joy?
How does God's joy over His people inspire my worship?
